message.content:
<deliberation>
Step-by-step approach:
1) Parse scope and task
- Main nodes to evaluate: REG-031 (Art13(7)(vi)), REG-051 (Art26(9)), REG-009 (Art9(7)).
- Compare each node (with its cross-references) against the entire policy chunk.
- Identify deviations only where there is a discernible difference (narrowing, broadening, altered method, timing, responsibility, etc.). Treat omissions as Non-Deviation per rule 4.

2) Identify and load cross-references
- REG-031 (Art13(7)(vi)) is part of Article 13’s instructions-for-use content. Relevant contextual refs: Art13(1)-(6), (8) to understand transparency, instructions content, and adjacent items (REG-025, REG-026, REG-027, REG-028, REG-029, REG-030, REG-032).
- REG-051 (Art26(9)) explicitly references Art13. Consider Art13(1)-(6), (8) (REG-025, REG-026, REG-027, REG-028, REG-029, REG-030, REG-032).
- REG-009 (Art9(7)) references Article 60. Consider Art60(1)-(9) (REG-055 to REG-063).

3) Compare each regulation with policy text
Policy chunk summary:
- Requires defining/documenting data requirements relative to intended uses, stakeholders, geography; documenting in an Impact Assessment.
- Requires defining/documenting procedures for data collection/processing (annotation, labeling, cleaning, enrichment, aggregation).
- If using existing datasets to train, assess quantity/suitability and document in Impact Assessment.
- Define/document methods for evaluating data; evaluate all datasets and document results.

REG-031 (Art13(7)(vi)):
- Regulation expects, when appropriate, that instructions for use include specifications for input data or relevant info about training/validation/testing datasets, considering intended purpose.
- Policy requires comprehensive internal data governance and documentation (in an Impact Assessment), evaluation methods, and assessment of datasets. It does not contradict or negate the regulatory expectation; it does not claim that such specifications will not be provided to deployers or in instructions for use. Any absence of explicit “instructions for use” language is an omission → Non-Deviation.
- No narrowing of scope (policy covers data types broadly and evaluation of all datasets). No altered timing or order, no negation, no responsibility shift that conflicts with the Article.

REG-051 (Art26(9)):
- Regulation: Deployers shall use information under Art13 to comply with DPIA obligations under GDPR Art35 or LED Art27.
- Policy: Mentions “Impact Assessment” and documenting requirements/assessments therein; it does not say deployers will/not use Article 13 information for DPIA. No contradiction or negation found. The absence of an explicit DPIA mention is an omission → Non-Deviation.
- No timing, responsibility, or execution change that contradicts the regulation.

REG-009 (Art9(7) + Art60):
- Regulation allows testing procedures to include real-world testing in accordance with Art60.
- Policy does not preclude real-world testing; it says nothing about banning such testing. Omits real-world testing specifics → Non-Deviation.
- No contradictory statements about approval, consent, oversight, or reversibility required by Art60.

4) Conclusion
- For all three nodes, no explicit contradiction or alteration of constraints was identified in the policy chunk. Omissions are treated as Non-Deviation per rule 4.
- Include considered cross-references explicitly in output.
</deliberation>

    "deviation_found": false,
